name: Chestnuts in Cointreau Syrup
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup Cointreau or Other orange-flavor liqueur
recipeInstructions:
Grand Marnier, Triple Sec, etc.
1. Chop each chestnut in half with a cleaver or heavy knife (lay them flat side down on a cutting board for easy chopping).
2. Place the nuts in a saucepan, add water to cover by 1 inch, and bring them to a boil over high heat.
3. Boil the nuts, uncovered, for 3 minutes, then drain them.
4. Pry or pop the halves out of the shells. The skin will usually remain in the shell; scrape off any bits that adhere to the nuts.
5. Halve each piece and return the nuts to the saucepan. Add the skin of one tangerine and enough cold water to cover the nuts.
6. Bring the liquid to a boil, lower heat, and simmer the nuts gently, partly covered, until they are translucent (about 1 hour). If necessary, add more boiling water to keep the nuts covered.
7. Using a slotted spoon, lift the nuts from the cooking liquid, leaving any scum behind. Discard the liquid and peel.
8. Combine the sugar, water, and remaining tangerine peel in the saucepan; bring the mixture to a boil.
9. Simmer the syrup for 10 minutes, then add the chestnuts. Bring the syrup back to a boil again, then lower heat and simmer the chestnuts, covered, for 15 minutes.
10. Remove any pieces of zest and return the syrup to boiling. Simmer it for an additional 3 minutes.
11. Ladle the nuts and syrup into a sterilized storage jar and add orange liqueur. Cover the jar, shaking it to mix everything, then cool and refrigerate. The flavor improves for several weeks. It stores for up to a year in the refrigerator.
12. For serving, spoon this sauce into stemmed glasses and top with lightly whipped cream, or serve as an ice cream topping.
Yield: About 2 1/2 cups
